change applies.

4 Quality Information Document for OMI_CIRCULATION_VOLTRANS_ARCTIC_averaged 
 
© EU Copernicus Marine Service – Public   
CMEMS-ARC-OMI-QUID-CIRCULATION 
Issue 4.2 – March 2026 
1) INTRODUCTION 
a) Product Name  
 
OMI_CIRCULATION_VOLTRANS_ARCTIC_averaged 
 
b) Indicator Evaluation Method 
The Atlantic Water volume transport time series provided here represents the integrated 
ocean volume transport through the Faroe -Shetland Channel, Barents Sea Opening 
and Fram Strait. The indicator timeseries are validated using in -situ based observations 
found in literature. For details, see the Ch. 3.2 of the Ocean State Report #3 (von 
Schuckmann et al., 2018).  Here, Atlantic Water is  defined using the following 
temperature criteria: T > 5 °C (Faroe-Shetland Channel); T > 3 °C (Barents Sea Opening); 
T > 2 °C (Fram Strait). The locations of the sections are shown in Figure 1. The temperature 
criterion applied excludes any transport of colder water masses of Arctic origin in the 
northern parts of the sections. Moreover, most of the water masses transported by the 
Norwegian Coastal Current in the southern part of the section are also excluded. The 
volume transport is expressed in Sverdrups (Sv; 1 Sv = 106 m3s-1).  
The overflow waters  volume transport  time series provided here represents the 
integrated ocean volume transport through the Faroe -Shetland Channel and  the 
Denmark Strait that meet the density criteria (σθ > 27.8 kg m-3).

2) SCIENTIFIC VALIDATION  
a) Nordic Seas volume transport 
  
The positions of the sections in the reanalysis data are taken such that it matches the 
location of standard hydrographic sections that are also monitored by arrays of current 
meter measurements. In the Nordic Sea
